goal: Generate History Go place quizzes where local history and place stories come first, humor/quirks second, concrete place facts third, and fag/emne analysis only after the historical and place-near material is exhausted. Theory may guide the generator internally, but user-facing questions must never read like syllabus or metadata quizzes.
core_principle: local_history -> stories/people/buildings/events -> humor/quirks -> concrete place facts -> stedskarakter -> spørsmålsvinkel -> question; emner and theory are support layers and late-set drivers, never the visible motor in early sets.
blocked_formulations: Hva gjør dette stedet relevant for
Når dette stedet analyseres gjennom fagplanen
Hvorfor er dette et viktig grøntområde
Hvordan fungerer dette stedet som byrom
Hvilket fagbegrep beskriver best dette stedet
Hvorfor er dette stedet relevant for
Hva gjør dette stedet relevant for biologisk mangfold
Hvordan fungerer stedet som eksempel på
Hvilken teoretiker er særlig nyttig når
Hvilken topic hook fra fagkartet passer best når
Hva gjør stedet til et eksempel på

gjenkjenning.purpose: Identify the place through a concrete signature trait.
good_openings: Hva ved stedet gjør det lett å kjenne igjen
Hva er det første som skiller
knowledge_requirements: must name a concrete place trait
must add why the trait matters in the city

historisk_endring.purpose: Frame the place through change over time.
knowledge_requirements: must mention at least one dated or periodized shift
must connect shift to current place character

teknisk_fysisk.purpose: Ask about construction, infrastructure, material or physical solution.
knowledge_requirements: must include a concrete technical or material detail
must explain what that detail enables

bruk.purpose: Ask about actual use, rhythms and user groups.
good_openings: Hva brukes stedet til i dag
knowledge_requirements: must describe actual use today
must avoid generic byliv wording

romlig_lesning.purpose: Read movement, openness, closure, direction and position in space.
good_openings: Hva gjør at stedet åpner seg
knowledge_requirements: must describe a spatial effect
must tie it to movement or orientation

saertrekk.purpose: Distinguish the place from similar places in Oslo.
knowledge_requirements: must state a real differentiator
must not fall back to generic category labels

kontrast.purpose: Use contrast against a nearby or comparable place.
good_openings: Hva er den tydeligste forskjellen mellom
Hvilket trekk gjør at dette ikke fungerer som
knowledge_requirements: must compare against an explicit contrast target or same-type place
must explain difference, not just name it

tidslag.purpose: Read multiple periods or surviving traces in the same place.
good_openings: Hvilke epoker kan fortsatt leses her
knowledge_requirements: must identify at least one surviving trace
must connect trace to present-day reading

begrepsdefinisjon.purpose: Define or identify a core concept that actually helps read the place.

begrepskontrast.purpose: Distinguish between two close concepts, terms or analytical pairs.

begrepsanvendelse.purpose: Apply a concept to the place instead of only defining it abstractly.

question_families.story: Question built around a memorable local story, turning point or anecdote tied to the place.
question_families.kuriositet: Question built around an unexpected, quirky or surprising detail that makes the place memorable.
question_families.person: Question anchored in a person strongly tied to the place through use, design, event, statue, institution or memory.
question_families.hendelse: Question anchored in an event, happening, controversy, opening, accident, performance or public moment at the place.
question_families.scene: Question anchored in a vivid place-situation the player can imagine or observe on site.
question_families.visuell_hook: Question anchored in something visually striking, bodily noticeable or easy to point at in the place.

opprinnelse.purpose: Ask what was here before the current place existed.
good_openings: Hva startet historien til stedet med
knowledge_requirements: must identify an earlier state
must connect old state to the present place

endring.purpose: Ask what changed the place and why it matters.
good_openings: Hva gjorde at området ble noe annet
knowledge_requirements: must identify a concrete shift
must explain why the shift matters

bygning.purpose: Ask about the history, role or transformation of a concrete building or built structure tied to the place.
knowledge_requirements: must mention a concrete building or structure
must connect it to local history

humor.purpose: Use playful but real local details, quirks or odd contrasts without becoming silly.
good_openings: Hva legger folk ofte merke til
knowledge_requirements: must be rooted in a real place detail
must still teach something true about the place

analytisk_tolkning.purpose: Ask a deep, place-specific analytical question informed by emner and theory, without exposing generator metadata.
knowledge_requirements: must stay anchored in the place
may use academic language but not meta-fagplan wording

set_specific_rules.1: Knowledge should foreground local history and a vivid story detail.
set_specific_rules.2: Knowledge should tie people, buildings, events or former functions to the place.
set_specific_rules.3: Knowledge may be lighter or more playful, but must still teach something true and place-specific.
set_specific_rules.4: Knowledge should deepen concrete facts through building history, visible traces or why the detail matters.
set_specific_rules.5: Knowledge should deepen the concrete place story through institutions, functions, people or development lines.
set_specific_rules.6: Knowledge may be more academic, but should explain the place through analysis rather than syllabus language.
set_specific_rules.7: Knowledge may be advanced and theory-shaped, but the visible question should remain about the place.
set_specific_rules.8: Knowledge should define the concept clearly and show why it helps interpret this place.
set_specific_rules.9: Knowledge should foreground conflict, rupture, power or contested change in local history.
set_specific_rules.10: Knowledge should synthesize rather than quiz theory names.

output_mapping.dimension: Derived from angle_rules.dimension_hints and/or emne dimensions.
output_mapping.topic: Concrete place topic in sets 1,2,4; faglig topic in sets 3,5,6.
output_mapping.emne_id: Optional support field in sets 1,2,4; required and load-bearing in sets 3,5,6.
output_mapping.related_emner: Fill from emne relations and mapping context, especially in sets 3,5,6.
output_mapping.core_concepts: Required in sets 3,5,6 when concept-bearing emner are used.
output_mapping.concept_focus: Required in set 6; optional but useful in sets 3 and 5.
output_mapping.tags: Use place_type, subtype, set mode and major thematic markers.
output_mapping.question_scope: place for sets 1-5; emne for sets 6,7,10; concept for set 8; place for set 9.
output_mapping.difficulty: Low in set 1, low-to-medium in set 2, mixed in sets 3-7, conceptually hardest in set 8.
output_mapping.question_type: Prefer history/fact/story/person/event/scene in sets 1-5; deeper analysis only from set 6 onward.
output_mapping.source: Must list the concrete sources, files or place/emne evidence used to draft the question.
output_mapping.targetId: For by place quizzes, copy placeId into targetId on every question and at file level.
output_mapping.question_layer: Required. Stamp from set mode. Sets 1-3 may share lighter layers; sets 4-5 place_advanced; sets 6-7 emne layers; set 8 concept.
output_mapping.theory_ref: Required in sets 4, 7 and 8. Must trace the question to fagkart.topic_hook canon via topic_hook_id, thinker_id, thinker_name, work and theory_focus.
output_mapping.topic_hook_id: Required in sets 4, 7 and 8. The emne-driven question must be traceable to the exact fagkart topic_hook that supplied the canon thinker(s).
output_mapping.thinker_name: May be surfaced in question, options and/or knowledge; in sets 4, 7 and 8 the named thinker should be visible often enough that the player actually learns the fagplan canon.

park.extra_rule: Do not let two parks in the same batch share the same subtype+primary family combination.

knutepunkt.extra_rule: Force at least one question on flows, transfers or waiting.

gate.extra_rule: Prefer street character, commerce, rhythm or transformation over generic byrom wording.

boligomrade.extra_rule: At least one question should distinguish the area from another housing type in Oslo.

plass.extra_rule: At least one question should address the place as a scene, threshold or civic room.

omrade.extra_rule: Prefer large-scale area logic over single-building detail.

generator_pipeline: 1. Read place, quiz_profile, desc, popupDesc and all place-profile extensions.
2. Run history detector and place-content analysis before choosing size.
3. Choose the smallest strong profile the material supports.
4. Build sets 1 and 2 from local history, buildings, events, former functions and named people.
5. Build set 3 from quirks, humor, visual hooks and memorable everyday-life details.
6. Build sets 4 and 5 from concrete, verifiable place facts, architecture and development lines.
7. Only if the place still carries enough strong material, unlock emne sets 6-8 and optional major sets 9-10.
8. Use emner, mapping and theory_ref internally to deepen analysis, but strip all visible meta-fagplan wording from question text and options.
9. Validate against banality guard and shorten the quiz if weak filler appears.
10. Emit production-ready adaptive set file and flat export if needed.
validator_checks: Each set has between 5 and 7 questions.
Set 1 and set 2 must always exist.
If set 6 exists, sets 1-5 must already be strong and non-banal.
No question may mention topic hook, fagplan, fagkart, canon or mapping logic in visible question text.
No question may ask which theorist is most useful or best fits the place.
Early sets must be history-first and place-first.
At least 4 strong local-history/building/event questions must appear across sets 1 and 2.
At least 2 humor/quirk/curiosity questions must appear by the end of set 3 when set 3 exists.
If material cannot support strong early sets, shorten the quiz instead of keeping analytical sets.
Questions must remain place-specific, concrete and non-banal.
targetId must equal placeId on every question for by place quizzes.
question_layer must exist and match the active set mode.

minimal_place.use_when: Place has limited but usable history and concrete local material. No emne sets required.

normal_place.use_when: Place supports strong history and place material, but not enough for full emne expansion.

rich_place.use_when: Place can carry long local history plus late analytical sets.

major_place.use_when: Place is historically dense enough for deep conflict/history and final analytical synthesis.

binding_set_structure.binding_rule: History comes first, humor/quirks second, concrete facts/buildings third, and fag/emner only after that. Small and medium places may end before any emne set appears.

1.description: Open with local history, origin and the strongest place story.

2.description: Buildings, former functions, named people and events.

3.description: Playful, memorable and still true local details, quirks, scenes and everyday life.

4.description: Concrete, verifiable place facts about buildings, materials, architecture and visible details.

5.description: Deeper concrete reading of the place through conflict, functions, institutions and development lines.

6.description: First analytical set. Academic and deep is allowed, but the question must still be place-specific and not meta-syllabus.

7.description: Advanced analytical set. Theory may structure the question invisibly, but the visible wording must stay on the place.

8.description: Concept set. Concepts must help read the place, not float free or become school-style metadata.

9.description: For major places only: wars, demolition, political conflict, contested planning or ruptures in local history.

10.description: For major places only: synthesize history, structure and concepts without quizzing theory names directly.

file_shape.targetId_rule: File-level targetId must equal the placeId / place target of the full quiz file.

question_shape.targetId_rule: For by place quizzes, targetId must equal placeId on every question. It is required even when the file-level targetId is present.
question_shape.question_layer_rule: Every question must carry a question_layer that matches the set mode. The generator may not leave this inferred or blank.

theory_ref_shape.rule: Use fagkart topic_hook canon only. thinker_id must exist inside the canon.thinkers array of the referenced topic_hook.

question_shape_notes.question_scope: Use place in sets 1-5 and 9; emne in sets 6,7,10; concept in set 8.
question_shape_notes.question_type: Keep early sets historical, vivid and concrete. Analytical and concept-heavy types start late.
question_shape_notes.source: Early sets should cite concrete place-near source material when possible.
question_shape_notes.question_layer: Bind question_layer to the adaptive set map in v4.3.
question_shape_notes.targetId: Repeat the place target on every question to match the superset quiz schema and simplify downstream filtering.
question_shape_notes.topic_hook_id: Required in sets 4, 7 and 8. Trace every theory-bearing question to a specific fagkart topic_hook.
question_shape_notes.theory_ref: Required in sets 4, 7 and 8. Use it to store the named theorist/work that the question teaches from the fagplan canon.

sets_1_5.rule: Early and middle sets must originate from local history, buildings, events, people, humor, visible details or concrete place facts.

sets_6_10.rule: Late analytical sets may be emne-driven, but the visible question must still be written as a place question, not a metadata question.

authoring_workflow: Build sets 1 and 2 from local history first: earlier functions, buildings, named people, conflicts, former uses and dated shifts.
Build set 3 from quirks, humor, scenes, visual hooks and everyday life, while staying true to the place.
Build sets 4 and 5 from concrete verifiable details, building history, architecture, institutions and development lines.
Only after the history/place core is strong may the generator unlock set 6 and beyond.
Use emner and theory to sharpen late questions, but never expose topic hook or fagplan wording in the visible question.

optional_fields_for_entertaining_sets.stories: Short local stories, anecdotes or turning points that can seed set 1 or 2 questions.
optional_fields_for_entertaining_sets.fun_facts: Quick surprising facts or memorable details.
optional_fields_for_entertaining_sets.related_people: Named people strongly tied to the place.
optional_fields_for_entertaining_sets.related_events: Events, openings, protests, accidents, concerts or public happenings tied to the place.
optional_fields_for_entertaining_sets.visual_hooks: Things players can immediately see, notice or imagine on site.
optional_fields_for_entertaining_sets.quirks: Oddities, paradoxes, nicknames, slang or peculiar local traits.
optional_fields_for_entertaining_sets.institutions: Institutions, venues, buildings or organizations that matter for the place story.
optional_fields_for_entertaining_sets.artifacts: Statues, signs, infrastructure, artworks or objects that can anchor concrete questions.
optional_fields_for_entertaining_sets.building_stories: Short stories about specific buildings, structures, former functions or visible traces.
optional_fields_for_entertaining_sets.local_conflicts: Conflicts, controversies, demolition debates, planning battles or class tensions tied to the place.

place_profile_extensions.fallback_rule: If these fields are missing, the generator must derive equivalent material from desc, popupDesc, signature_features, must_include and mapped quiz context before falling back to emner.

source_pool_rules.rule: For sets 1, 2, 3, 5 and 6, the generator must exhaust concrete place-near source pools before drawing on emne wording or abstract analytical language.

4.rule: Set 4 must resolve each emne-driven question through a topic_hook and named theorist.

7.rule: Set 7 must deepen emne questions through theory and named theorists from fagplan canon.

8.rule: Set 8 must connect concepts to theories/theorists from the fagplan canon rather than stay glossary-like.

entertainment_rules.surprise_value_definition: A question has surprise value if the answer or knowledge contains a memorable, unexpected or vivid detail likely to stick with the player.
entertainment_rules.bonus_info_definition: Knowledge should often contain one extra detail, contrast, anecdote or sensory cue beyond what is needed to justify the answer.
entertainment_rules.size_rule: Early-set quality outranks quiz length. A shorter vivid quiz is better than a full padded quiz.

superset_sync.purpose: Align quiz generator output contract and validator with the canonical question superset while keeping the v4 place-first 8-set logic.
key_changes: targetId added as required field on each question
question_layer added as required field on each question
field order aligned more closely with supersetQUIZMAL
validator checks added for targetId and question_layer consistency
adaptive set count and question count added in v4.2
sets 9 and 10 added for major places

field_binding_rules.targetId: Must be emitted on every question and must equal the place target for by place quizzes.
field_binding_rules.question_layer: Must be emitted on every question and must match the adaptive set map in v4.3.
field_binding_rules.question_scope: Must match set mode and stay in sync with question_layer across the 8-set structure.
field_binding_rules.topic_hook_id: Required in sets 4, 7 and 8 and must resolve to a real fagkart topic_hook.
field_binding_rules.theory_ref: Required in sets 4, 7 and 8 and must match a canon thinker in the referenced topic_hook.
field_binding_rules.adaptive_profile: Every generated file must store or log the chosen adaptive profile in generation metadata, even if the downstream quiz format does not expose it.

fagplan_theory_integration.binding_rule: In sets 4, 7, 8 and 10 the generator must teach emne + theory + named theorist from the fagplan. Emne-only academic questions are not sufficient.
fagplan_theory_integration.lookup_chain: place -> mapped emner -> fagkart.topic_hooks where emne_id appears -> canon.thinkers -> theory_ref
preferred_surface_forms: name theorist directly in question text
name theorist in one or more options
compare two theorists when the hook canon supports it

theory_ref_example.work: The Social Life of Small Urban Spaces
theory_ref_example.theory_focus: observasjon av faktisk bruk av plasser

quiz_size_rules.principle: Quiz size must follow real historical and local material. A place without enough stories, buildings, events, conflicts or memorable details must get a shorter quiz instead of emne padding.

minimal_place.use_when: Place has limited but usable history and concrete local material. No emne sets required.

normal_place.use_when: Place supports strong history and place material, but not enough for full emne expansion.

rich_place.use_when: Place can carry long local history plus late analytical sets.

major_place.use_when: Place is historically dense enough for deep conflict/history and final analytical synthesis.

profile_selection_hints: Choose the smallest strong profile the material can actually carry.
Minimal and normal places may end before any emne set appears.
Do not unlock emne sets unless the history/place material is already strong.
Major profile requires deep local history, not just many emner.

place_content_analysis.purpose: Estimate how much strong quiz material the place actually has before choosing set count and question count.

history_detector.goal: Find local history, building history, former functions, conflicts, named episodes and visible traces before any emne logic is used.
history_detector.rule: If fewer than 4 strong local-history candidates exist, the generator must not unlock long history or emne-heavy profiles.

banality_guard.principle: If the generator cannot produce strong local history, vivid place stories and concrete building/event questions, it must shorten the quiz instead of padding with obvious or pseudo-academic filler.
hard_rules: If fewer than 4 strong local-history candidates exist, do not force full history architecture or emne expansion.
If early-set candidates are mostly obvious landmark questions, reduce quiz size.
If candidate questions rely on generator vocabulary such as topic hook, fagplan or teoretiker, reject them.
If emne sets begin to sound like metadata quizzes, shorten the profile or rewrite them as place-specific analytical questions.
Never keep an emne set alive if the history and place sets are weaker than the analytical tail.
banal_question_signals: question asks about topic hook, fagplan or mapping instead of the place
question asks who is the most useful theorist
question only paraphrases a visible landmark
question asks isolated year/name/height without context
question sounds like a dry emne heading turned into a question
question contains one absurd distractor and two empty ones
fallback_action_order: replace with stronger local history, building, person or event question
move analysis into knowledge instead of the question stem
merge material into fewer but better sets
reduce questions_per_set by 1 within allowed range

generation_metadata_rules.generation_profile_rule: Store the chosen adaptive material profile (minimal_place, normal_place, rich_place or major_place) in generation metadata whenever possible.

adaptive_activation_rules.rule: Core sets must exist in every by quiz. Optional sets may only be activated if the place has enough verified material.

user_facing_question_rules.principle: Questions must read as real history/place questions, not as quiz-generator metadata or school-syllabus prompts.
must_do: Pack concrete local information into the question stem when relevant.
Prefer stories, buildings, events, people, conflicts, traces and named local details.
Let analytical depth appear through place-specific wording, not through explicit references to topic_hook, fagkart or fagplan.
must_not_do: Do not ask which topic hook fits the place.
Do not ask which theorist from the syllabus is most useful.
Do not expose generator metadata such as topic_hook_id, canon, theory_ref or mapping logic in question text or options.
user_facing_question_rules.theory_visibility_rule: Theory may live in hidden metadata and can shape the framing, but the visible question must remain about the place, its history, buildings, events, users, conflicts or concepts.

content_priority.rule: When material is scarce, drop later layers first. Never sacrifice history to keep emne/theory sets alive.

theory_usage_rules.principle: Theory is a drafting engine, not the visible quiz object.
forbidden_visibility: question text framed as syllabus metadata
options that ask which theorist or topic hook fits best
questions that expose canon or mapping names
theory_usage_rules.rule: The generator may use theory_ref internally to shape depth and precision, but must translate it into richer place-specific questions.
